behavior_summary: Provides the gameplay behavior for obsidian coin items: when a coin stack is inspected ('U8P_COINS::look'), the script builds a human-readable string containing the stack count and the phrase ' obsidian coin' and makes the owning process/actor speak that string to the viewer.
Separately, a helper updates the coin item's visual frame based on quantity: quantities 1..5 map to frames 0..4 and any quantity >= 6 maps to frame 5.
Temporary strings are allocated (numToStr, concat) and freed after use; quantity is read from the item via Item::getQuantity and used both for wording (pluralization) and for frame selection.
notable_control_flow: U8P_COINS::COINS::look: read quantity -> convert number to string -> concat ' obsidian coin' -> if quantity != 1 then concat 's' -> call Item::bark with constructed string.
U8P_COINS::COINS::combine: read quantity -> if quantity < 6 set frame = quantity - 1 else set frame = 5 -> call Item::setFrame.
Short ephemeral control sequences (implies/pop) are used around the bark call; they appear to be control/flow artifacts and do not affect the constructed message semantics.
open_questions: Which PID is passed into COINS::look as the bark target (inferred from [BP+06h] but the surrounding caller context is not shown)?
Are there any additional side-effects or state flags set elsewhere when combine runs (none observed here, but external calls could have side effects)?
Exact memory ownership/allocator semantics for temporary strings (numToStr/concat/free) — the script frees temporaries but precise allocator rules are not shown.
Whether pluralization rules extend beyond simple '!= 1' (e.g., localization or special cases) — here only a binary plural suffix is used.

main_function.name: U8P_COINS::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_COINS::look' (inspect) event for coin items: read the stack count, construct an English description like '<N> obsidian coin(s)', and have the owning process/actor speak it via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_COINS::look) — triggered when the item is examined/inspected.
Function is entered with the item 'this' pointer in [BP+06h] (inferred).

function_map.name: U8P_COINS::look
function_map.role: Builds and emits the coin-count description when the item is examined (Item::bark).

function_map.name: U8P_COINS::combine
function_map.role: Update the item's display frame based on quantity (maps 1..5 to frames 0..4, 6+ to frame 5).

function_map.name: Item::getQuantity
function_map.role: Return the numeric quantity of the item (used by U8P_COINS::look and combine).

function_map.name: U8P_COINS::numToStr
function_map.role: Convert a numeric quantity to a decimal string for message construction.

function_map.name: U8P_COINS::concat
function_map.role: Concatenate strings to build the spoken description.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit a short text message from an item/process to a target pid (used to show the 'U8P_COINS::look' text).

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Set the item's visual frame/index (used by combine).

external_calls.purpose: Read the stack count to determine wording and pluralization.
Convert the numeric quantity into a string for concatenation into the output message.
Append the literal ' obsidian coin' and conditionally 's' to the numeric string.
Have the owning process/actor speak the constructed description string to the viewer pid.
Read the stack count to decide which visual frame to set.
Set the sprite/frame index based on the quantity mapping (1..5 -> 0..4, 6+ -> 5).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the gameplay behavior for obsidian coin items: when a coin stack is inspected ('U8P_COINS::look'), the script builds a human-readable string containing the stack count and the phrase ' obsidian coin' and makes the owning process/actor speak that string to the viewer.
